summary: This is a Transfidelity project update document from Cerebrum DAO describing early-stage development of compounds to prevent protein misfolding in neurodegenerative diseases, specifically targeting alpha-synuclein aggregation in Parkinson's disease. The document outlines current experimental activities, near-term milestones through December 2025, and planned validation studies in early 2026 using disease-relevant cell models.

content_blocks.title: NEWS FROM THE LAB!
content_blocks.content: Introduction: With $FIDEL launched, the Transfidelity team is developing compounds that could prevent protein misfolding in neurodegenerative diseases. Document indicates the team is making impressive progress on compound development.

content_blocks.title: WHAT'S HAPPENING NOW
content_blocks.content: Current experimental activities: (1) Testing improved versions of original compounds while validating findings across different cell types - indicates iterative compound optimization and cross-validation in multiple cellular systems. (2) Developing a disease cell model using stem cells that overproduce alpha-synuclein, the protein that clumps together in Parkinson's disease - indicates establishment of Parkinson's-relevant cellular disease model using stem cell technology to recapitulate alpha-synuclein pathology.

content_blocks.title: COMING UP IN THE NEXT FEW MONTHS
content_blocks.content: Near-term milestones (by December 2025): (1) Complete testing of optimized compounds, (2) Finish developing the Parkinson's disease cell model, (3) Pathway analysis to understand mechanism of action of compounds. Timeline indicates structured experimental plan with specific deliverables.

content_blocks.title: Early 2026 Plans
content_blocks.content: Planned validation studies in early 2026: (1) Testing whether compounds can prevent toxic protein clumps (alpha-synuclein aggregates) from forming in Parkinson's neurons - critical functional assay for therapeutic efficacy, (2) Deeper molecular analysis through transcriptomics (gene expression profiling) and proteomics (protein expression analysis) - indicates plans for comprehensive mechanism of action studies. Document notes team is moving faster than expected on several fronts, described as encouraging for early-stage research.

key_findings: Project is developing small molecule compounds targeting protein misfolding in neurodegenerative diseases, specifically alpha-synuclein aggregation in Parkinson's disease
Currently at TRL 2-3: conducting compound optimization and validation across different cell types, with iterative testing of improved compound versions
Developing disease-relevant cellular models: stem cell-based models that overproduce alpha-synuclein to recapitulate Parkinson's pathology
Timeline: December 2025 milestones include completing optimized compound testing and finishing Parkinson's disease cell model development
Early 2026 plans include critical functional validation: testing ability of compounds to prevent toxic protein aggregate formation in disease-relevant neurons
Mechanistic studies planned: pathway analysis (near-term) and comprehensive transcriptomics/proteomics analysis (early 2026)
Project pace is ahead of expectations, indicating good execution in early-stage research
No quantitative efficacy data, animal model data, regulatory pathway information, or IP status provided in this update

milestones.title: $FIDEL Token Launch
milestones.description: Launch of the $FIDEL token by the Transfidelity team, marking the beginning of the current phase of research activity.
milestones.source_quote: With $FIDEL launched, the Transfidelity team has been making impressive progress on developing compounds that could prevent protein misfolding in neurodegenerative diseases.
